wordplay_note: An everyman reduced to a chip of a person — impoverished and diminished
card_description: Joe Chip (meaning "Chip = microchip / small piece") is a character in Philip K. Dick's *Ubik*. Technician and protagonist who discovers he may be in half-life after a bomb explosion. Name note: An everyman reduced to a chip of a person — impoverished and diminished. The little man struggling against entropy and corporate control of reality. Appears in 3 Exegesis passages. First appears in *Ubik*. Context: "The author writes to Claudia, interpreting the "decay backward of forms" seen by the character Joe Chip in the novel *UBIK* as movement along the "orthogonal" axis of time."
